items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Warrior, please allow me to introduce myself. I am Verbia, an apprentice Gladiator.

options.text: You've been through a lot, Verbia.
You've done well, Verbia.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: No, it was my weakness that led to all of this.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Medulius dangled "glory" like bait. He turned our slaughter into a feast. It's nobles like him, drunk on bloodshed, who are the real undying monsters of Septimont.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I'm the fruit of a rotten root. I always knew I couldn't outrun what made me… But I still clung to false hope, too afraid to face the shadow cast by Septimont's glory.

options.text: The current Ephor has ended the blood duels.
Septimont has begun to face its own darkness.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Bloody Agon is no more. Anyone who disgraces the arena's honor, noble or not, will face justice.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: What a great time you're in... it makes me want to cry.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Warrior, if you get the chance… please deliver a message to the current Ephor. The fameless Gladiator Verbia offers her deepest respect.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Beyond that gate is my father.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I won't fight him again. Not for spectacle. Not to replay the same cruel farce for heartless eyes. So please, face him in my place. Give him the final duel he deserves.

options.text: You have my word.
It would be my honor to duel him.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Thank you... truly.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Who... are you?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Does it matter?

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Why did you... save me?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: A daughter just lost her father. There's no need for a father to lose his daughter too.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I lost.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You didn't even strike.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I couldn't.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Do you know what a musician does if she plays the wrong note?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: She plays on. No matter how broken, how off-key, the melody never stops.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Then what should I do... I must... save him...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Phrolova stares at Verbia in silence, perhaps out of weariness from scenes like this.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Or perhaps, she's waiting for the young woman to "fall into the trap" herself.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: ...Name your price, witch.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You didn't even ask what I could offer.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Doesn't matter. I'll pay. Whatever it takes.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Heh.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Then let's make a deal. I'll seal your father within the shrine, where he shall wait for a true warrior to grant him a final, glorious death.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: As for you, I'll use the Lumenstones as anchors of my Orchestration to reshape your body. With a few notes of dissonance, of course.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: The Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Yes. You will be a piece of music composed by the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: This pact will hold until the last Lumenstone goes dark. I wonder how long you'll endure the Dark Tide's corruption. A day? Or a century?

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I'm a Gladiator of Septimont. The Dark Tide doesn't hunt me. I hunt it.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: We'll see, Gladiator. We'll see.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Only Honeycakes and no Saltimbocca in your pack? Explain that, Verbia.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I didn't have the time...

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Don't be picky with your food.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: It's not that. I came running the moment I heard where you were, so I...

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Take my share, then. You fight better when you eat well.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Yes, sir.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: And eat the Bloodleaves, too. The hunt isn't over. You'll need every ounce of strength.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Sir Aulus... no, Father. I want to end this hunt.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Give me your reason.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: The old tales got it wrong. The Dark Tide's curse isn't about undying monsters. It's about us... creatures of the Dark Tide who keep coming back.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Tell me, Verbia. Why are we here?

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I know what you're getting at. No one believes in a Gladiator who's lost their honor. Those Phantoms... they're warriors who chose to become myth.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Their thirst for redemption gave form to the "undying monster."

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: You have your mother's wisdom.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: And I have you to guide me.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Remember, Verbia, a true Gladiator can hunt even what cannot be seen.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: And you, my daughter, will become one.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Father?

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: I've been here too long... long enough for the Dark Tide to sink its teeth into me. But you still have a way out.
Aulus's body begins to distort, twisted by the corruption of the Dark Tide. The proud Gladiator becomes something less than human.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: If you want to reclaim your glory, you must slay the undying monster.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: So come. Hunt me.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: No...

items.speaker: Aulus?
items.text: Who's there...

options.text: {PlayerName}, the fameless Gladiator,
{PlayerName}, champion of the Great Agon.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: By the request of apprentice Gladiator Verbia, I challenge you to a duel.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Ah?! Excuse my lack of propriety, {PlayerName}. I am Aulus, disgraced champion of the Great Agon.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: By the request of apprentice Gladiator Verbia, I challenge you to a duel.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Verbia... my daughter... is she safe?

options.text: She fell in battle. A hero, to the very end.
She's alive.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Dark Tide didn't take her. She held on until I arrived.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: There should be no lies between Gladiators, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...The Dark Tide didn't take her. She held on until I arrived.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Verbia, I'm proud of you. Now, it's my turn to fight.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: And mine as well. Hee hee...
Medulius emerges from the shadows, his body twisted and reshaped, completely consumed by the Dark Tide. He's become its twisted apostle.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: {PlayerName}, I followed your suggestion. This time, I'm standing inside the arena!

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Medulius! How dare you desecrate this duel!

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Oh, what a terrible misunderstanding. Both of us have been embraced by the Dark Tide, haven't we? We're already one and the same.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Come on, then! Tear each other apart!

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Keep your stance firm and steady your breath, Verbia. Never let your enemy see you falter.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Yes, Coach— I mean, Aulus.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: …Coach... Father. Aulus. Doesn't matter what you call me. No matter how hard I trained you, you never made it as a real Gladiator.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: You had the makings of Septimont's sharpest spear… but you cracked in the final trial.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: It was my love for you that ruined you.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I'm sorry, Aulus.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Gladiators don't say sorry.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Yes, sir.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: We speak with steel. We dance with death. We live and fall for glory.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Yes, sir.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Ever since that match… I've carried nothing but regret. Maybe it was how I taught you that made you falter.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: No! You were the best coach in all of Septimont!

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Not anymore. My brightest student lost her nerve and stained the arena with shame.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I didn't...

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: You lacked the resolve to grant your opponent the death they deserved.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: That's not true!

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Then why did you hesitate? Why did you spare your opponent? Why did you disgrace the arena of glory?

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Because my opponent... was you!

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: That day wasn't supposed to end like that. We were supposed to finish sparring and go home like always…

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: I couldn't kill my own coach in my debut match… and my father shouldn't die at his own retirement ceremony… I... I just couldn't…

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Wipe your tears. They cloud your vision.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: Yes...

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: Verbia, my dear Verbia. A Gladiator's glory is not their own. It's given by the crowd. That day, they were ready. The arena was ready.

items.speaker: Verbia
items.text: But I wasn't, Father. I wasn't ready.

items.speaker: Aulus
items.text: There are still Lumenstones unlit and Tacet Discords still stalk the dark. We'll keep hunting until the day you're ready to reclaim your glory.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Lighting the Lumenstones also stirs Phantoms of the past.)

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Phrolova... hahaha! That witch promised me the grandest duel Septimont has ever seen.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: And she wasn't lying! Not one bit! You're the strongest contender I've ever seen!

options.text: I'm not here for your clown show.
You should be grateful you saved Lenore back when you'd forgotten your memories.

items.text: With a clean strike, you bring an end to Medulius, who dissolves into water that seeps into the cracks of the shrine.
He lets out an unsettling laugh as his body dissolves into dark water, seeping into the cracks between the shrine's stones.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Even in the darkest depth of Septimont, they couldn't escape it. Fighting was always their fate.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Glory binds them to the arena. And the love they had for each other turned to sharpened blades... How marvelous!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: How could you say that, Mr. Medulius?!

options.text: Is this what you wanted? Families harming each other?
You're watching them like they're your dessert.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Exactly.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: The more Lumenstones you lit, the more of myself I remembered. Now I know why I lingered in this abyss.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: This! This right here, is what I want. To witness Septimont's greatest duel again and again!

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: A champion fated to fall by the hand of his daughter, his brightest student, his sharpest spear! That's the kind of battle Gladiators were born for!

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: Why did you turn so cold the moment your memories came back? You risked your life to save me, Mr. Medulius! You weren't like this before!

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Oh, Miss Lenore... I was once a warrior, one who stood by the Sentinel's side. Only those who give everything for glory earn a place in Septimont.

options.text: That's just an excuse for bloodlust.
It's not worth people's lives.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You only played noble because you weren't the one in the arena.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Now, now.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: No need to take my words too seriously. Think of me as a fly, one you can swat whenever you please.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: I'll be waiting before the shrine. Light the final Lumenstone.

items.speaker: Lenore
items.text: Mr. Medulius! When did you get here?

options.text: Stay behind me, Lenore.
You broke your promise, Medulius.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: No need for swords and scowls, {Male=Mr.;Female=Ms.} {PlayerName}. I didn't come by choice. The Lumenstone brought me.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: When you lit it, something stirred in me. All those long-lost memories came flooding back.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Ah, how could I ever forget that day?

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: The day the legendary Gladiator Aulus announced his retirement and chose his final opponent—his own daughter, Verbia, the finest student he'd ever trained.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: It was meant to be a gentle farewell match. Something symbolic. But they clashed like fire and steel. It electrified the crowd, and it electrified me!

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: I remember leaping from the upper stands and elbowing my way to the front row. I remember Aulus's spear—it missed my face by inches. And then the blood hit the air. So sharp and intoxicating, like a newborn's first inhale of life.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: It could have been a match for the history books! If only Verbia hadn't flinched at the end. She ran. Killed the mood for everyone. A real shame.

options.text: You sound disappointed.
At least no one was hurt.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Honestly, yes. I am disappointed.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: But tragedy or triumph... that's for the spectators to decide.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: W-wait! I'm Medulius, an Elder of Septimont's Senate! Whatever you're after, I can get it for you!

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Oh? I didn't think it would be this easy.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: So it is me you're after? Then you must know the connections I have in the Senate. Just say the word and I'll...

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You're here to watch the duel, aren't you, Medulius?

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: Y-you knew...?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Seems you're dimmer than most villains, and twice as reckless as your average halfwit.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: But since you've been so generous offering yourself to me as a vessel, I'll grant you a wish in return.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: A... wish?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I'll let you live long enough to see your little duel. So long that you'll be begging for death before the end.

items.speaker: Medulius
items.text: No... no!
